_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in using various online tools for open-source research (e.g., online trade, DNS, mail, etc.).
reference_document_element_description: Skill in producing analysis documentation that is reproducible, clearly reasoned, and accessible to a non-technical business audience.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-003 (TF, cosine: 0.482), OP-S-010 (OP, cosine: 0.470).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.5/10 (raw 0.352). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DCWF-only terms [dns, etc, mail, online, open, research] vs WIDAI-only terms [accessible, analysis, audience, business, clearly, documentation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.861 (P=0.855, R=0.866). Balanced P/R — the 15-word DCWF element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.5%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in using various online tools for open-source research (e.g., online trade" and "Skill in producing analysis documentation that is reproducible, clearly reasoned". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.503 > STS 0.352 (gap +0.151). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.2%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DCWF-only: [dns, etc, mail, online, open, research]. WIDAI-only: [accessible, analysis, audience, business, clearly, documentation].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.5% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.151 with low STS 0.352. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
